title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: A novel about a library between life and death, where every book represents a different life you could have lived — and the choices that shape who we become.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship that teaches developers how to write readable, maintainable, and efficient code through practical principles and real-world examples.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: An iconic exploration of cosmology, black holes, and the origins of the universe, written for general readers by one of the greatest scientific minds of our time.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A philosophical novel following a young Andalusian shepherd's journey to find treasure, discovering along the way the true meaning of destiny and the language of the world.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt & David Thomas
description: A timeless guide for software developers covering career development, coding best practices, and the mindset required to become a truly effective and adaptable programmer.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A sweeping narrative of humanity's history from the Stone Age to the present, examining how biology, culture, and ideas have shaped the human species.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A powerful story of racial injustice and moral growth in the American South, seen through the eyes of young Scout Finch as her father defends a Black man wrongly accused of a crime.

title: You Don't Know JS
author: Kyle Simpson
description: A deep-dive series into the core mechanisms of JavaScript, challenging developers to truly understand the language rather than just use it on the surface level.

title: The Gene: An Intimate History
author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
description: A compelling history of the gene and genetics, tracing its discovery from Mendel's peas to CRISPR, and exploring profound ethical questions about identity and the future of humanity.

author: George Orwell
description: A chilling dystopian novel set in a totalitarian future where Big Brother watches every citizen, exploring themes of surveillance, propaganda, and the fragility of truth and freedom.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
author: Martin Kleppmann
description: An essential guide for engineers on how to build reliable, scalable, and maintainable systems, covering databases, distributed systems, encoding, and stream processing in depth.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: A groundbreaking work in evolutionary biology arguing that genes, not organisms, are the fundamental units of natural selection — introducing the concept of the 'meme' along the way.
